name: Manna BBQ
notes: All-you-can-eat Korean BBQ with table grills and neon vibes. Can hit a 2-hour wait on weekends — they call your number so you can walk around Westgate.

name: Hapa Food Company
notes: Hawaiian plate lunch spot. The galbi ribs and garlic shrimp are the move. Malasadas are legit — get the ube.

name: Thai E-San
notes: Solid neighborhood Thai. Pad Thai and spicy basil dish are both great. Thai tea is excellent.

name: Allora Gelato
notes: Voted best dessert worldwide and it shows. Rotating seasonal flavors. Mango Sticky Rice and Croatian Fig are standouts.

name: Quik Burrito
notes: Great breakfast burrito. Special chipotle sauce is great. Strawberry horchata

name: Aftermath Restaurant
notes: The Aftermac (mac and cheese) is the move. Pasta is great too. Happy hour menu is worth a trip on its own.

name: Bellair Golf Park
notes: Newly renovated par-3 course with fresh sand in the bunkers and great greens. Driving range fills up fast.

name: Andiamo Pizzeria
notes: Huge slices. Margarita pizza is perfect — thin crust, good cheese-to-sauce ratio. Late-night spot near Madison Square Garden.

name: Thai Coconut
notes: Underrated family Thai. Pad Woon Sen, papaya salad, and yellow curry are all solid. Small place, popular for takeout.

name: Jokers Ice cream And Italian Ice
notes: Small shop with a wild flavor selection. Bourbon caramel and cherry jubilee combo is a winner. Owner Shannon is great.

name: Green Corner Restaurant
notes: The chicken shawarma sandwich is huge — easily splits two people. Al Sham beef sandwich is a regular-order. Fries are great.

name: Hush Public House
notes: The Italian beef oxtail sandwich and the date cake. $55 for 3 courses on Tuesday

name: Chantico Mexican Restaurant Phoenix
notes: Crispy beef tacos are the move. Start with the Saguaro Cooler cocktail. Outdoor sunset dining is really good.

name: Angie's Prime Grill
notes: Build-your-own burrito counter with tons of options. Surf and turf combo is popular. Kiosk ordering, no table service.

name: The Perch Brewery
notes: Aviary slash brewery — rescue birds in the courtyard. Beer flights are great. Burgers and wings are reliable. Caesar salad is surprisingly good.

name: Spitz - Desert Ridge Marketplace - Restaurant and Bar - Mediterranean Food,
notes: Street Cart Fries are the standout — keep an eye on your order. Doner bowls and wraps are solid fusion Mediterranean.

name: Dilla Libre Uno
notes: Great quesadilla

name: Hyderabad House Arizona - Scottsdale
notes: Great Indian spot in Scottsdale. Biryani is excellent — the Vijayawada chicken biryani specifically. Gongura curry is a must-try. Lunch specials are a deal.

name: Crescent Ballroom
notes: Intimate downtown Phoenix music venue. Great for seeing up-and-coming artists up close — no barriers between you and the stage. Craft cocktails and cider on tap.

name: Si Señor Restaurant of Arizona
notes: New Mexican spot with green and red chile. Hatch Valley combination plate is excellent. Watermelon margarita. Gorditas plate is great.

name: Biryani and Bites
notes: Great indian

name: Lupita's Hot Dogs
notes: Bacon-wrapped Sonoran hot dog with guacamole, salsa, and onions. Soft bun. Open late.

name: Tacos Calafia
notes: Al pastor and cabeza tacos are the ones to get. They throw in roasted jalapeños and onions at no charge.

name: Jj's Gyros
notes: Chicken Shwarma Plate is great! Dont get desserts.

name: Glai Baan
notes: Amazing food. Get a reservation. Get the dumplings

name: Volanti Restaurant & Lounge
notes: Airport restaurant with runway views — sit outside if you can. Reuben is great. $100 burger is a thing. Great for layovers.

name: Chino Bandido
notes: Mexican-Chinese fusion counter service. Jade Red quesadilla is a highlight. Black beans are better than the refried.

name: Carolina's Mexican Food - Cactus
notes: The Carolina's burrito is the go-to. Local chain that's been around forever.

name: Casa Corazon Restaurant
notes: The shrimp culichi is the standout — multiple people call it the best thing they've ever had. Chile nogada is rare and worth ordering. Full self-serve salsa bar.

name: Aioli Gourmet Burgers
notes: Aioli burger with sweet potato fries is solid. Staff will walk you through the menu. Creative milkshakes.

name: Ramen Kagawa
notes: Really nice Japanese curry

name: Born & Bred, Scottsdale
notes: Pizza looks insane. Chicken Caesar wrap at lunch. Their ranch dressing is amazing

name: Guido's Chicago Meats & Deli
notes: Meatball sub was great. Chicken cutlet caprese sandwich - add prosciutto

name: Salsitas tacos al carbon
notes: Birria tacos are solid. Horchata is great at this location specifically.

name: Namaste Indian Restaurant
notes: Family-owned Indian spot. Chicken makhani and chicken tikka masala are well done. Lamb vindaloo is lean and properly portioned. Spice levels run hot — worth flagging if sensitive.

name: Geordie's at Wrigley Mansion
notes: Insane views of Phoenix. Go at sunset

name: Toulouse Petit Kitchen & Lounge
notes: Delicious. French onion soup. Desserts were amazing

name: Aunt Chiladas Squaw Peak
notes: Long-standing Mexican spot in a hacienda-style building. Mini chimis are great. Flour tortilla chips. Outdoor bar and live music on weekends.

name: Jalapeño Inferno, Pinnacle Peak
notes: Flour tortilla chips

name: El Encanto Dos
notes: Outdoor patio is really nice. Good for a relaxed dinner. Pollo fundido is worth trying.

name: Four Peaks Brewing Company
notes: Airport Four Peaks location. Jalapeño cheeseburger is solid. Enchiladas are good. Outlets at the tables.

name: HULA'S Modern Tiki Uptown
notes: Solid happy hour

name: Barrio Café
notes: Featured on Triple D and it earns it. Not typical Mexican — creative and bold. Guacamole is great. Halibut is a regular recommendation.

name: Nippon Curry featuring Hinoya Curry
notes: Great Curry. Pork Katsu

name: Mensho Tokyo SF
notes: Wagyu Ramen

name: La Taqueria
notes: Carne Asada Burrito. Super. Dorado Style. Side of Salsa Roja. Horchata. They accept credit cards now.

name: Ajo Al's Mexican Cafe
notes: Family-owned since 1986 in the Biltmore neighborhood. Queso dip and fajitas are the move. Dog-friendly patio. Chimichanga with grilled steak and chicken is great.

name: Jacky's Mexican Food
notes: Menudo and pozole are solid for brunch. The salsa is excellent — reportedly drinkable. Horchata is smooth.

name: Lylo Swim Club
notes: Adult dole whip. Chips and salsa. Creamy

name: Fry Bread House
notes: James Beard award winner. Native American food.

name: Original Hamburger Works
notes: Burgers cooked over charcoal

name: Flaming Pig
notes: Solid filipino food

name: Hamburguesas Y Cervezas
notes: Mexican inspired hamburgers. Would recommend the al pastor wings. $4 margaritas on happy hours

name: Throne Brewing & Pizza Kitchen
notes: Very good mac and cheese. Not a bad place to meet before a Suns game.

name: Palma
notes: Asian food. Chili garlic noodles. Potato scallion waffle. Lots of great stuff.

name: Otro Cafe
notes: Cafe de olla

name: UnderTow
notes: Fully immersive tiki bar built to look like the underbelly of an old-world ship — inside Century Grand in Phoenix. Cocktail menu is serious. One of the best tiki bars in the country.

name: Paradise Valley Burger Company
notes: Featured on DDD. The Booze Burger is the move — beer-battered patty, not greasy. French toast fries are sweet and wild.

name: Kitsune Brewing Company
notes: Tan tan beer. So good

name: Soul Cafe
notes: Steak and eggs are great. Banana bread is a highlight. Patio has a grotto vibe. Gets a wait on weekends.

name: Taco Chelo
notes: Fish tacos with pickled onion and cream sauce are the standout. Fresh tortillas and beans. Roosevelt Row taqueria with craft cocktails.

name: Elmer's Tacos
notes: Best bean and cheese burrito

name: The Stand Arcadia Burger Shoppe
notes: Drive thru only. The fries were good and crispy. GREAT dipping sauce. Would of liked to try a shake or lemonade

name: Ziggys Magic Pizza Shop
notes: Pizza shop with hidden pinball arcade. Has small parking lot

name: Matt's Bar and Grill
notes: Featured on DDD. Matt's Big Breakfast — great downtown Phoenix breakfast spot. Chicken sausage is good. Big team-service vibe, always friendly.

name: Call Her Martina
notes: Elote is great, drinks are good. Bit pricey - but thats the general area.

name: Liz Modern Asian
notes: Michelin-quality Chinese-Asian in Scottsdale. Shrimp noodles and orange chicken are the go-to. Restaurant week lunch special is a steal at $22/person for 7 courses.

name: AZ Kabob House
notes: Persian family-owned spot. Koobideh kebab and baghali polo with lamb are excellent. Lamb gyro plate is solid and portions are generous.

name: Bánh Mì Bistro Vietnamese Eatery
notes: Good Bahn mi sandwich. Nice bread

name: Little Miss BBQ-University
notes: Central Texas-style BBQ. Brisket is the star — tender and smoky. They only make a set amount per day and close when it's gone. Get there early.

name: Neighborly Public House
notes: Cornbread was great. Nice patio

name: Wong's Restaurant
notes: Great Chinese food. Been there a long time. Friend rice with the BBQ pork. Orange chicken

name: Captain Scott's Lobster Dock
notes: Lobster rolls at a take-out window with picnic table seating. Fish and chips get serious praise. Fried scallops are great.

name: Yutaka Japanese Restaurant
notes: Spicy miso ramen is the reliable order. Tonkatsu ramen is also solid. Small spot — come before the dinner rush.

name: Lake Pleasant Brewing Company
notes: Worth the drive. German beers are the specialty and always clean. Good rotating food trucks.

name: Tortas CM
notes: Chicken torta is the move — chicken cooked with vegetables so the flavor soaks in. Specialty sauces include spicy ketchup. Parking can be tricky.

name: Dessert in Desert
notes: Asian-inspired desserts in North Scottsdale. Egg waffles with ice cream and fish cones with custard. Taro ice cream is next level. Tornado potatoes are a must.

name: Katy's Dumplings - Westmont
notes: Great beef noodle soup

name: El Güero Canelo Restaurant
notes: Sonoran hot dogs — bacon-wrapped, sold out of a strip-mall spot. Reviews are mixed on consistency.

name: Girl & The Goat
notes: Call ahead, order the full goat experience. Very Cool. TONS of food, bring a group.

name: Gallo Blanco
notes: Barbacoa Tacos. Al Pastor Tacos. Chelada. Key Lime Tart.

name: Señor Bob's Hot Dogs
notes: Good hotdogs. Better fry's. Honey mustard is really good. Big portions

name: Fat Olives
notes: Featured on DDD. Great neopolitin pizza. Got the lasagna and the pizza with calibrian Chiles.

name: Richardson's Restaurant
notes: Great new Mexican food

name: Taco Boy's
notes: Great taco spot. $2.69 per taco. 1lb meat and 15 tortillas was $30. Great salsa bar. Got the Barbacoa

name: Pemberton PHX
notes: Very hip spot. Want to try the pasta food truck. Lots of great seating. Food trucks and cocktail trucks. Great spot to meet freinds

name: Slanted Rice Vietnamese Bistro
notes: Featured on DDD. Sweet pork crispy wonton spring roll is great. Soups are fresh and well-made.

name: República Empanada Restaurant
notes: Great empanadas. Awesome margarita. Small place, can get real busy.

name: Chelsea's Kitchen
notes: Canal-side patio in an old house conversion. Cornbread is moist, tacos are flavorful. Dog-friendly heated patio. Grilled steak has great char.

name: Steak 44
notes: Awesome steaks

name: Hopdoddy Burger Bar
notes: Good burgers and shakes

name: Trevor's Liquor
notes: Really awesome liquor store with patio.

name: My Slice of the Pie Pizzeria
notes: Al pastor and carne asada pizza. Lots of pizza with Mexican influence

name: Chula Seafood Uptown
notes: Good seafood. Cool atmosphere, not fancy. Fish tacos are huge. The salmon is crispy. AJs is next door for dessert

name: Rewined Beer And Wine Bar
notes: Amazing 90's themed bar. Incredible interior. Insane beer selection

name: Flo's
notes: Really good Chinese food

name: The Porch
notes: Mozzarella sticks are a go-to. Good pub food, extensive menu. Happy hour is okay — nothing special on pricing.

name: Harvey's Wineburger
notes: Great dive bar, no windows. More parking in the back. French Fries are great.

name: Ted's Hot Dogs
notes: Charcoal-grilled dogs from a Buffalo original that's been around since 1927. Line forms fast. Epic side portions. Worth checking out the shakes.

name: Monroe's Hot Chicken
notes: Best chicken sandwich in downtown Phoenix according to multiple people. Craft sodas are good. Near the Suns arena. Parking is rough — walk or light rail.

name: Gadzooks Enchiladas and Soup
notes: Build-your-own enchiladas and tacos, Chipotle-style but way better quality. Festival tacos come with the 50/50 corn-flour tortillas — get those. The medley of fresh ingredients actually slaps.

name: Uncle Biff's California Killer Cookies
notes: Walk in and they hand you a free sample. Classic homemade-style cookies — chocolate chip, holiday flavors, the works. Tastes like grandma's kitchen but in a strip mall.

name: Le Relais de l’Entrecôte
notes: Fixed menu only — salad, then steak frites. That's it. The secret green pepper sauce is the whole point. They bring you half first, then keep the rest warm on a hot plate for seconds. Long queue outside before they even open.

name: Valley Wings
notes: Total hole-in-the-wall. Mango habanero wings and the buffalo loaded fries are the move. The Kool-Aid is somehow also great. Ask for extra sauce.

name: Tacos Chiwas Chandler
notes: Chihuahua-style tacos with a corn-flour hybrid tortilla you won't find anywhere else in the valley. Go for the gorditas. The meat comes in actual chunks — not the usual mystery shred.

name: Los Taquitos
notes: Long-running family spot. The pozole hits and the breakfast burritos are legit. Chicken fajitas are a go-to. The vegetarian taco meal is a sneaky good option.

name: Two Hands Seoul fresh corn dogs
notes: Korean corn dogs you can customize. Most are around $5-6. Add cheese if you want. The tater tots are worth getting as a side.

name: Convenient Corner Market Deli
notes: 24-hour corner store with a deli in the back. The pastrami sandwich is legit — one reviewer literally stopped mid-bite to review it. Ruben is also solid but pricey.

name: Grocery Station
notes: Safeway deli. Known for the fried chicken being fresh in the morning. Basic grocery store deli situation.

name: My Spot Mediterranean Grill
notes: Kosher Mediterranean spot. Shawarma is the move — perfectly seasoned and tender. First-time falafel? This is where to try it. Friendly staff, fresh fries.

name: Pepper Lunch
notes: Japanese quick teppanyaki food.

name: Il Forno Bello Pizzeria
notes: Run by the guy that worked at Pizzeria Bianco for like 32 years.

name: El Chullo Peruvian Restaurant & Bar
notes: The lomo saltado here is supposedly the best version in the valley — multiple people who've been to Peru say it's that good. Make a reservation, it gets busy. Pisco sour is solid too.

name: The Ends
notes: Bison rib eye

name: Taco Guild
notes: Set inside a restored old church with stained glass windows and vaulted ceilings — the space is wild. Sustainable Mexican fare, great habanero salsa. Strong drinks. Craft beer and tequila program.

name: IncaMex
notes: Peruvian food

name: Tempe Eats Food Hall & Bar
notes: Fish Pop - fish and chips. English dude said they are amazing

name: Lumsum Thai Cuisine
notes: Authentic Isaan Thai. The boat noodles and crispy pork are standouts. Live traditional instrument on weekends. Anything with the dipping sauce is a must.

name: Shaghf Cafe
notes: Cookie bowl looks insane

name: California Burger & Breakfast Company
notes: Diner that does everything — burgers, fish and chips, Sonoran dogs, Mexican food. Zucchini fries are made in-house and worth ordering. Hot wings are huge and meaty.

name: T Swirl Crepe
notes: Japanese-style crepes. Self-serve ordering kiosks inside. The Angus short rib crepe is one of the more interesting options. Mango crepe is huge and stuffed.

name: Mendocino Farms
notes: Creative gourmet sandwiches with seasonal ingredients. The chicken pesto caprese is a winner. Busy at dinner — online order might run long.

name: El Super Taco
notes: Shrimp taco is the standout when they have it. Asada burrito is solid and filling. Salsa bar is the move. Quality has fluctuated but on a good day it hits.

name: Baragi
notes: Korean spot that's only been open a bit but people are already going back multiple times a week. Comes with tons of banchan side dishes. Affordable and authentic.

name: JJ's Deli
notes: Great looking sandwich place. Suggested to try Santa fe Chicken

name: Pinnacle Brewing Company
notes: Scottsdale brewery doing more traditional styles — Czech Amber Lager and Dark Lager are the sleepers. Beerded Barbecue food truck on-site daily. Big patio.

name: Khải Hoàn Restaurant
notes: Old-school Vietnamese spot people have been going to for decades. Pho #2 and the Crisp Rice Noodle #45 are the orders. Fresh herbs, egg rolls are incredible. Best-priced pho in the area.

name: Nami Korean Kitchen & Sushi
notes: Want to try the rose Tteokbokki

name: La Casa DeLa Poblanita
notes: The Cemita sandwich looks amazing

name: Raj & Rani's Indian Kitchen
notes: Home-style Punjabi. Tarka dal and butter chicken are solid. Malai kofta is apparently the best version around here. Order medium spice — it runs hot.

name: Suspiros Glendale
notes: Mexican cake spot. Tres leches is the signature — people drive across town for it. Carrot cake and piña colada slice are also worth getting.

name: Tabla Indian Restaurant - Phoenix, AZ
notes: South Indian style — different from the typical valley Indian spots. Gongura Chicken Curry is the standout. Kadai Chicken is good too.

name: Sushino Mexican Roll
notes: Mexican-Japanese fusion rolls. The Cielo mar y Tierra roll and the carnívoro roll are fan favorites. Waffle fries are apparently some of the best around.

name: ONYX Ice Cream
notes: Ube soft serve

name: Gracie's Tax Bar
notes: Chopped cheese and pickle fries look great

name: Da Vàng Restaurant
notes: Best pho in Arizona according to a lot of people. Huge portions, no upcharge to substitute. Spring rolls are legit — rice paper doesn't fall apart. Small parking lot.

name: Well Seasoned
notes: Bayou bowl looks good

name: Philly Steaks & Burgers Co.
notes: The Sonoran Philly is the order. Sweet potato fries are great. French Dip is also solid. Local spot that doesn't try to be anything it's not.

name: MidEast Eats
notes: Family-run Mediterranean. Shakshuka and kefta are the things to get — not just the standard gyro. The woman at the counter will walk you through the menu.

name: Aja Catering
notes: Suggested by Jignesh

name: UNiQ Burger
notes: 100% vegan smash burgers that fool meat eaters. Chicken ranch wrap might be better than the burgers. Fries are impossible to resist. A little pricey but quality is obvious.

name: LaCo Smashburgers & Tacos
notes: People are driving 45 minutes for these smash burgers. Crispy edges, right sauce ratios — one guy called it a 10/10 on a random Tuesday lunch. Tequila and scotch selection too.

name: Pizza To The Rescue
notes: Pizza and puppies. Chicken Parm with pasta on a fajita plate looked great

name: Kris' Fresh Frybread
notes: Native American frybread spot. Green chili wamich and red chili wamich are both solid. The O'odham taco is worth trying. Owner/chef greets everyone who walks in.

name: Tea Light Café
notes: Vietnamese spot with a tea focus. Pork chops are reportedly some of the best around — tender, paired with crunchy cabbage. Banh mi chicken sandwich is well assembled. Lunch special comes with pho and spring rolls.

name: Brick & Barley - A Neighborhood Bar & Grill
notes: Whitney wings

name: Jalapeños Sonora Grill
notes: Yelp's #6 taco spot in the US. The Sonoran taco is the one to get. Solid salsa bar with pico, cabbage, carrots, lime, and atolía. Aqua fresca is also good.

name: Tikka N Taco
notes: Mexican-Indian fusion. Shrimp coconut curry and the paneer biryani are the standouts. Tamarind sauce with samosas is a must. The shrimp tacos use fresh ingredients and it shows.

name: The Taco Spot - North Phoenix
notes: Birria and carne asada burritos — both big and filling. Tablet ordering at the tables. Mixed reviews on the birria specifically.

name: Revolución Tacos + Cantina
notes: Speakeasy taco spot tucked in an alley off Central, easy access from the light rail. Quesadilla is the move — better than the tacos. $5 beers, big outdoor seating area.

name: Los Cuatro Nietos AZ
notes: Family-run spot, Alberto and his wife are super welcoming. Birria tacos and the homemade salsa verde are the things people rave about. Menudo is also on point.

name: Xolo
notes: Chips and souiza to start. Chipotle chicken enchiladas with the house sauce are over the top. Chile relleno is cooked right. Local spot that feels like you're eating somewhere only locals know.

name: la bamba mexican grill restaurant
notes: Everything made fresh to order including chips and guacamole. Six taco combo is the move. Small place, mostly eat-and-go. Aguas frescas are great.

name: La Purisima Bakery 3
notes: Best tortillas in town according to regulars. Green pork tamales are full of flavor. Conchas are solid. The cream horn is a sleeper hit.

name: Wally Burger
notes: Superhero-themed burger spot with arcade games playing Marvel and DC movies. Chili cheese fries are a solid value. Fun for kids, food is decent.

name: De Babel
notes: Ranked third in the nation for Mediterranean in 2024. Shawarma is the main event — juicy, well-seasoned. Get the fries extra crispy. Huge portions, bring people. The Tractor lemonade is also good.

name: Achiote
notes: Venezuelan and Mexican fusion

name: Condesa Phoenix
notes: This used to be the restaurant at Crescent Ballroom

name: Cactus Blossom Coffee
notes: Coffee shop inside a game store — you can browse while you wait. Salted maple latte and the espresso lemonade are the orders. Americano with raspberry and rose syrup is also solid.

name: Chin Up Donuts
notes: Deep fried cinnamon roll

name: Tell Your Friends
notes: Upscale cocktail bar with live entertainment. Oysters and steak for dinner if you want to commit. Happy hour is great — reportedly $15 on a $40 scotch drink. Glam vibe that actually delivers in person.

name: George Yang’s Chinese Cuisine
notes: Crab puffs are some of the best around — stuffed with real crab. Hot and sour soup is excellent. Kung Pao steak is solid. Reliable Chinese in a wood-paneled, relaxed space.

name: Suss Pastries
notes: The pop tarts and honey pecan cookie are the signatures. Cheesecake is one of the better ones in Phoenix. Tiny spot hidden in Orangewood Estates — owner greets you at the door.

name: Malegria Latin cafe
notes: Latin cafe with a walk-up patio. Fish tacos are the thing people come back for. Abuelita mocha is solid. Pastelitos and chilaquiles are also crowd favorites.

name: The Stetson Social
notes: Neighborhood bar with solid burgers and a good Saturday brunch 10am-2pm. Fish specials rotate — barramundi when they have it. Pastrami burger is the main draw.

name: Bitz-ee Mama's
notes: Classic Mexican-American breakfast spot. Chorizo skillet is big and delicious — ask for refried beans added. Red chili con carne is fresh, not from a can.

name: Tarbell's
notes: Upscale Mark Tarbell spot. Mr. Fish (usually sea bass) and the Hamachi Crudo are the signatures. Beef carpaccio is excellent. Be prepared to pay — and they will charge you for extra toast.

name: Detroit Coney Grill
notes: Downtown Phoenix cheap eats institution. Triple burger is $5.45. Coneys with chili are the classic move — eat fast or the chili makes them soggy. Cheesy chili tots are also great.

name: Full Moon Izakaya
notes: Happy hour and reverse happy hour. Ramen is solid, sushi is good too. Crispy rice is legitimately crispy. Sapporo on tap. Baby octopus is fried perfectly.

name: Lutong Bahay Filipino-Asian Restaurant & Mini Mart
notes: Filipino restaurant and mini mart. The lumpia is apparently worth going back for the next day — someone bought 50 more. Lechon is also on point. Small and clean.

name: China Chili
notes: Chinese food. Peking duck with rolls looks great. Get the wonton soup

name: INDIBAR
notes: Upscale Indian with Michelin-level ambition. Vegetarian chef's tasting menu is a thing. Raji papdi chaat is incredible. Full wine list. Beautiful space, on the expensive side.

name: Mercado Y Carniceria El Rancho
notes: Mexican market and butcher. The burritos are apparently the move — someone said they'd kiss the cook. Fresh meat and produce. Prices are a bit higher than a regular grocery store.

name: Bad Jimmy's Uptown
notes: Retro vibe smash burgers — red neon, 80s music, skylight. Waffle fries are great. Lettuce wrap instead of a bun is available. One of the better burger spots in Uptown.

name: Persepshen
notes: Full from scratch kitchen

name: Sala Thai Restaurant
notes: Pad Kee Mao is the dish to get — one reviewer said it's their favorite version across the whole country. Massive authentic Thai menu. Very consistent.

name: Ben Brothers Market
notes: Corner store with a Chinese kitchen inside. Buffalo chicken wings and the egg rolls are better than most actual Chinese restaurants. Been around since 1988. Shrimp fried rice is the classic order.

name: Chispa's Fast & Fresh
notes: Drive-through Mexican. Birria tacos on Tuesday and Wednesday are the move. Breakfast burritos are solid any time of day — chorizo and egg hits. Drive-thru is convenient.

name: Popo's Fiesta Del Sol
notes: Happy hour margaritas are $7 from 3-6pm. Rolled quesadillas are a standout. Chips come out of the fryer hot. Machaca taco is good. Classic Tex-Mex cantina that does the basics right.

name: Taco Papi
notes: Carne asada tacos and the quesaburria tacos are the ones to get. Salsa bar available. Tiny walk-up spot with authentic decor. Margaritas took a few reminders to arrive.

name: Seven Brothers Burgers
notes: Hawaiian burgers

name: Knock Kneed Lobster
notes: Phoenix's oldest restaurant. Classic fish and chips, broiled cod, fried shrimp. Has been around since the 1940s. Casual seafood spot that keeps it simple.

name: Hachi Ramen
notes: People drive from Casa Grande for this. Spicy miso ramen is the go-to. Ask for extra chashu pork. Can be a wait on weekend nights. Presentation is impressive.

name: El Chapo Hot Dogs
notes: Obregón-style Sonoran hot dogs wrapped in bacon with cheese, mayo, and ketchup. The Mexican burger is also a serious contender — green chili, ham, bacon, cheese. Underrated spot.

name: Walter Where?House
notes: Art gallery and music venue with a bar. Sound system is on point. Cool place to catch a show — A-Trak has played here. Multiple spots to hang out throughout the space.

name: Glaze Donuts AZ
notes: Cronuts

name: PHXNQRA WINGS
notes: Order ahead online — 45 minute wait is normal and worth it. Lemon garlic with pineapple is the sleeper sauce. Mac and cheese has insane flavor. Parmesan fries are also solid.

name: Rinaldi's Italian Deli
notes: Meatball sub is the move — perfectly seasoned, great sauce, fresh bread. Italian combo sandwich has been a staple for 30+ years. Classic deli spot in Scottsdale.

name: Novel Ice Cream
notes: Donut ice cream

name: Indian Village
notes: Frybread taco is a must — light and fluffy fry bread with great toppings. Navajo taco is the signature. Guacamole is some of the best around. Gift shop attached with native jewelry.

name: Random AF Taqueria
notes: Birria grilled cheese and birria egg rolls are the standout orders. Food truck situation — located inside a party store. Birria tacos with consomé are solid. Horchata is great.

name: Thai Rama
notes: Been open 30+ years and people have been coming for decades. Spicy garlic chicken is the signature dish. Mango sticky rice for dessert. Consistently good, reasonable prices.

name: Cinnabon Swirl
notes: Cinnabon meets Carvel soft serve. The Bonini is the thing — soft serve swirled with cinnamon roll pieces. Nostalgic NYC dessert vibes according to one New Yorker.

name: Presidio Cocina Mexicana
notes: Mexican brunch. No paid gave it a 9.2

name: Bad Jimmy's
notes: Smash burgers. LA burg. On Mondays the have dry rub wings...

name: Theos Kitchen To Go
notes: Food truck with some of the best chicken wings in Arizona — the BBQ sauce is the thing. Chicken sandwich is also well seasoned. Generous portions.

name: Shio Ramen & Crudo
notes: $12 ramen for lunch

name: Arreburro Burritos+Quesadillas
notes: Stretchy homemade tortillas are the main thing here. Barbacoa burrito is a 10/10 according to regulars. Asado rojo quesadilla is the standout — rich and flavorful. Everything runs spicy.

name: Sourdough & Co.
notes: Bread is made in-house, which makes a difference. Brisket sandwich and garlic pesto turkey are solid. Clam chowder in a bread bowl is the move if you want soup. Fresh and consistently good.

name: Campbell's
notes: Green chili eggs

name: Sushi Sen Scottsdale
notes: Solid Japanese spot in Scottsdale. Known for the okonomiyaki, katsu don, and eel — apparently one of the few places in AZ doing okonomiyaki. Reserve if coming with 4+ after 6pm.

name: Apsara Asian Restaurant

name: Tia Carmen
notes: The ambiance of Tia Carmen is intentional in its efforts to honor the Southwest region and Mexico. As you walk through the large wooden doors, you're greeted by a down-to-earth vibe and quiet elegance. Chef Angelo Sosa wanted to recreate a reminder of his experience with his Aunt Carmen when he first learned food was his love language. The menu is not only a homage to his roots and to indigenous people but also a festival of culinary delights. Highlights include ember-roasted purple yam, chicken guisado, Baja striped bass, yucca brulee, and native grain fried rice. Ingredients are sourced locally, which reflects Sosa's desire to support the community. The entrees are complemented with craft cocktails, including the gin-based Hibiscus Desert Balloon, a yuzu and bergamot Paloma, and Mi Tia, a tropical drink that honors Sosa's family. ($$$)

name: Los Reyes De La Torta
notes: This thriving enterprise produces those giant Mexican sammies known as tortas. These are the Godzillas of the sandwich world, and could easily kick any ciabatta's butt, then go on to feed a family of six. The Torta del Rey is like a Mexican Dagwood, with fried beef, ham, egg, chorizo, avocado, and about a half-dozen more items, grilled and stacked high in a soft Mexican bread known as a telera. Los Reyes de la Torta also boasts a juice bar with all types of fresh-squeezed aguas frescas - lime, pineapple, mango, and so on. Or you can have six or more juices together in a colossal drink called a bomba, or add to the decadence level with a fruit and condensed milk creation called an agua cremosa. All of the fruit drinks come topped with little paper umbrellas, rounding out this colorful experience. ($)

name: Emilio’s Tacos & Hotdogs
notes: A sunny yellow-and-hot pink exterior, a flashing neon open sign and glowing window frames, and bright Loteria-inspired artwork hint at the explosion of flavors on deck at Emilio's Tacos & Hotdogs on 19th Avenue. Customers on the go can pick up their order at the drive-thru, but we recommend grabbing a table either inside or on the small patio to soak in the full experience. The Sonoran Hot Dog is the best we've found in Phoenix and comes with all the requisite ingredients, including beans, tomatoes, and mayo piled high on top of a bacon-wrapped hot dog. The tacos are great, the carne asada fries are a must, and the thick bright pink strawberry horchata is an unusual treat that straddles the line between drink and dessert. ($)

name: Recreo

notes: Old school Mexican food joint. Shrimp quesadilla with big tender shrimp gets mentioned a lot. Seafood-heavy menu alongside the classics.

name: Rice Paper Vietnamese Restaurant

notes: Some great looking Mexican seafood

name: Nello's Ahwatukee
notes: Some great looking pizza. Has Chicago deep dish

name: Comedor Guadalajara

name: Angelina's Fry Bread

name: CAVA

name: By The Bucket Gilbert
notes: Pasta in a bucket. Tuesday special is $7 and feeds two people easily. Garlic bread is huge.

notes: Eastern European bakery. Beef burek is flaky and juicy. Cevapi in pita with onions. The keks torta and tiramisu are worth grabbing on the way out.

name: Susie's Mexican Cafe and Lounge

name: Wingz And Thingz
notes: Soul food wings spot in Avondale. Came up on TikTok. Wings and collard greens get the love. Owner is hands-on.

name: My Mother's Restaurant
notes: Homestyle American. Grilled pork chop and the pasta with garlic bread. Pies. Been in the neighborhood a long time.

name: Lenny's Burger Shop
notes: Retro 50s diner with made-fresh burgers and malts. Everything is made to order so expect a wait. Good throwback vibe.

name: Mora Italian
notes: Elevated Italian osteria by Scott Conant. Chef's tasting menu is a good deal. Do reservations. Free valet.

name: The Stand
notes: Local burger stand. Standard Burger and the Arcadia are the go-tos. Chocolate milkshake is excellent. Drive-up only, no seating.

name: Original Golden Skone
notes: Fry bread sandwiches — kind of like a soft bready taco. The beef and bean skone topped with salsa, lettuce, and cheese is the one. Cherry and Nutella for dessert.

name: Laura's Burgers and More
notes: Chorizo burger

name: SugarJam The Southern Kitchen
notes: Southern brunch spot. Chicken and French toast combo is the signature. Lobster with creole shrimp. DJ on weekends — expect a wait.

name: Cocina Madrigal Tacos + Tequila
notes: Apparently some world class Mexican food. Currently operating out of a temporary food truck setup while the main location rebuilds. Homemade tortillas.

name: Papi Gordo's
notes: Birria tacos and the Korean taco both get shoutouts. Good house margaritas. Solid portion sizes.

name: Tratto
notes: Italian by Chris Bianco. Shared by NYT

name: eegee's
notes: Tucson original now in PHX. The eegee slushie is the whole point — orange dream is the flavor to get. Fries with ranch are a must.

name: Rocket Burger & Subs
notes: Burgers and 100s of different sodas. Huge selection of hard-to-find bottled sodas — buy 3 get 1 free. Chicken burger gets called out.

name: Via Della Slice Shop
notes: Best NY-style pizza in AZ according to people who've made the rounds. The Phoenix, pepperoni, and smoky potato slices. Vodka slice is a fan favorite.

name: Brunch & Sip
notes: From the owners of Los chicken and waffles, and Monroe's hot chicken. Pancakes are fluffy and legit. Lobster with creole shrimp. Expect a long wait on weekends.

name: AZ Lemonade Stand
notes: Local lemonade brand. Prickly pear and mango are the flavors to grab. Sold in jars — people take home a case.

name: The Meat Market
notes: Premium local butcher. Grass-fed beef, aged cuts, thick ribeyes. Staff knows their stuff. If you're doing carnivore this is your spot.

name: Morning Kick
notes: Known for breakfast burritos. Started as a food truck at the Gilbert farmers market. Cute little spot with game stations for kids.

name: Chou's Kitchen
notes: Northern Chinese in Chandler. Handmade dumplings and noodles — you can watch them make it. Soup dumplings are the reason to go. Gets a long wait.

name: Texaz Grill
notes: Texas-themed steakhouse that's been around 40 years. Brisket sandwich gets highlighted. Prime rib is the thing on special nights — call ahead.

name: Corleone's Philly Steaks & Pizza
notes: 2 for 1 on Tuesday. Get American cheese

name: The Great Gadsby Bakery
notes: English bakery with legit pastries. Pain au chocolat, green apple lattice croissant, banoffee pie, and the salted caramel brownie. Authentic English Sausage Roll.

name: Lukes Of Chicago
notes: Italian Beef

name: MacAlpine's Diner & Soda Fountain
notes: Try the phosphate soda. Old time diner

name: Vovomeena
notes: Brunch spot people drive two hours for the pancakes. Blueberry pancakes are the headline. Mexican Benedict on a sope is also a thing. Order before you get a table.

name: Raspa2 Jalisco
notes: Mexican snack spot attached to Tacos Jalisco. The mangonada gets big love. Elotes, aguas frescas, flans.

name: Tacos Tijuana
notes: Quesabirria tacos are what people come for. Authentic street taco spot. Outdoor seating area.

name: Tacos Chiwas
notes: Family-owned Chihuahuan-style tacos. Al pastor, pollo, and the Chiwas beef taco are the orders. Also has a location in the airport.

name: Liyuen
notes: Supposedly best chinese food in PHX

name: CIBO
notes: Italian in a 1913 bungalow in downtown PHX. Wood-fired pizza. The potato and sausage pizza gets singled out. Pizza Polpettine with ricotta-filled crust.

name: Ta'Carbon Mexican Grill
notes: Authentic Mexican street tacos with meats cooked over wood charcoal. Loaded salsa bar. Carne asada taco and the taco hazz. Always packed with locals.

name: SEOUL BBQ & SUSHI
notes: Korean BBQ with tabletop grills. AYCE option available. Cold noodles are a sleeper hit. Fun for groups.

name: Pappadeaux Seafood Kitchen
notes: Cajun seafood chain but a good one. The etouffee, oysters, and tagliatelle pasta get called out. Key lime pie is legit. Full bar.

name: Aloha Kitchen
notes: Hawaiian plate lunch spot that's been around since the 90s. Kalbi plate and the poke are the highlights. Jr Special is the go-to order.

name: Tampopo Ramen
notes: Small ramen spot, always busy. Tonkotsu broth is the reason to go — light but full of flavor. Come early with a big group. Try the appetizers.

name: Mark's Cafe
notes: Hotel cafe at a Marriott with a breakfast buffet and a weekend Bloody Mary bar. Views are solid. Good for a quick breakfast.

name: Mrs White's Golden Rule Cafe
notes: Soul food institution since the 1960s. Fried chicken with greens and cornbread. Fried catfish melts. Real mac and cheese with sharp cheddar. Kool-Aid is on the menu.

name: Essence Bakery Cafe
notes: French-style bakery cafe. Best macarons in Phoenix. The Gruyère croissants are a must. Pot pies and quiche too.

name: Ramen Dozo
notes: Hidden gem ramen in Tempe. $13 tonkotsu is one of the best deals in the valley. Miso and tonkotsu are the go-tos. Gyoza is solid too.

name: Johnny's Burgers & Dogs
notes: Local mom and pop burger spot. Double cheeseburger and the chicken sandwich both get love. Fry sauce. Fresh and made to order.

name: Lucky Boy
notes: Old school counter service burger joint open since 1951. The 3/4 lb fire-grilled burger. Simple, classic menu.

name: Bobby D's Restaurant & Lounge
notes: Neighborhood bar and grill. Grilled wings for 25 cents during NFL games. Been open 20+ years. Friendly spot.

name: Los Taquitos
notes: Long-running family Mexican spot. Pozole is what people talk about. Chicken fajitas and the chile relleno are solid. Carne asada quesadilla too.

name: Tacos Sahuaro
notes: Authentic taqueria with hand-made corn tortillas. The quesabirria tacos are crispy and good. Carne asada has real flavor. Been around 20+ years.
